annotations.label: Real analytic function on an open set

anchor.snippet: Formally, a function

annotations.note: `AnalyticOnNhd 𝕜 f s` over `𝕜 = ℝ` is the same notion (analytic at every point of `s`).

annotations.label: Real analytic function via Taylor series

anchor.snippet: Alternatively, a real analytic function is an infinitely differentiable function

annotations.note: `HasFPowerSeriesAt f p x` formalizes that `f` equals the sum of its (Taylor-like) power series locally.

annotations.label: Real analytic at a point

anchor.snippet: defined on some subset of the real line is said to be real analytic at a point

annotations.note: `AnalyticAt ℝ f x` is exactly the pointwise definition.

annotations.label: Complex analytic function

anchor.snippet: The definition of a complex analytic function is obtained by replacing

annotations.note: `AnalyticAt ℂ f x` instantiates the same definition over the complex field.

annotations.label: Analytic in an open set (complex)

anchor.snippet: In complex analysis, a function is called analytic in an open set

annotations.note: `AnalyticOnNhd ℂ f s` matches the complex-analytic-on-open-set notion.

annotations.label: Polynomials are analytic

anchor.snippet: All polynomials

annotations.note: `AnalyticAt.aeval_polynomial` shows polynomial evaluations are analytic everywhere.

annotations.label: Exponential function is analytic

anchor.snippet: The exponential function is analytic

annotations.note: `exp_analytic` (and `analyticAt_exp_of_mem_ball`) prove that `exp` is analytic at every point.

annotations.label: Trigonometric functions are analytic

anchor.snippet: The trigonometric functions are analytic on any open set of their domain

annotations.note: `analyticAt_sin`, `analyticAt_cos` (both ℂ and ℝ versions) are proven, along with `analyticOnNhd_cos` etc.

annotations.label: Natural logarithm is analytic on single-valued branches

anchor.snippet: The natural logarithm is analytic on any open set where its branch is single-valued

annotations.note: `analyticAt_clog` proves the principal branch of `Complex.log` is analytic on the slit plane; `analyticAt_log` covers real positive `x`.

annotations.label: Power functions analyticity

anchor.snippet: Power functions are analytic everywhere for non-negative integral powers

annotations.note: Mathlib has analyticity of integer/complex powers via `AnalyticOn.cpow`, `analyticAt_pow`, and `differentiableOn_zpow.analyticOnNhd`, though no single unifying statement matches the prose.

annotations.label: Special functions analytic on suitable domains

anchor.snippet: Many special functions are analytic on a suitable domain

annotations.note: Specific special functions (zeta, Gamma, hurwitzZeta, etc.) are shown analytic on their domains, but there is no overarching statement.

annotations.label: Algebraic functions are analytic away from poles and branch points

anchor.snippet: Algebraic functions are analytic away from any poles and branch points

annotations.note: Grep finds no general statement that algebraic functions are analytic off poles/branch points.

annotations.label: Absolute value is not analytic at 0

anchor.snippet: The absolute value function

annotations.note: No explicit statement `¬ AnalyticAt ℝ abs 0` was found.

annotations.label: Piecewise defined functions typically not analytic

anchor.snippet: Piecewise defined functions (functions given by different formulae in different regions) are typically not analytic

annotations.note: This is an informal heuristic with no corresponding theorem in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complex conjugate is not complex analytic

anchor.snippet: The complex conjugate function

annotations.note: No theorem asserting `¬ AnalyticAt ℂ conj z` appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Compactly supported smooth functions are not analytic

anchor.snippet: Other non-analytic smooth functions

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the bump-like `expNegInvGlue` is smooth but `¬ AnalyticAt ℝ expNegInvGlue 0`.

annotations.label: Equivalent characterizations of real analyticity

anchor.snippet: The following conditions are equivalent

annotations.note: Some characterizations (power series, smoothness with controlled derivatives via `ContDiff`/`AnalyticOnNhd.contDiffOn`) exist but no unified equivalence theorem was found.

annotations.label: Multivariable characterization of real analyticity

anchor.snippet: In the multivariable case, real analytic functions satisfy a direct generalization

annotations.note: Mathlib's `AnalyticAt`/`HasFPowerSeriesAt` is set up over arbitrary normed spaces `E`, so multivariable real analyticity is the same notion.

annotations.label: Sums, products, compositions of analytic are analytic

anchor.snippet: The sums, products, and compositions of analytic functions are analytic

annotations.note: `AnalyticAt.add`, `AnalyticAt.mul`, and `AnalyticAt.comp` (also `AnalyticOnNhd` versions) are all in `Constructions.lean`.

annotations.label: Reciprocal of a nonvanishing analytic function is analytic

anchor.snippet: The reciprocal of an analytic function that is nowhere zero is analytic

annotations.note: `AnalyticAt.inv` takes an analytic `f` with `f x ≠ 0` and gives `AnalyticAt 𝕜 (f⁻¹) x`.

annotations.label: Inverse of one-to-one analytic function is analytic

anchor.snippet: In one variable, the inverse of a one-to-one analytic function whose derivative is nowhere zero is analytic

annotations.note: Mathlib constructs formal left/right inverses of power series with positive radius (`leftInv`, `rightInv`, `radius_rightInv_pos_of_radius_pos`), but a direct theorem `AnalyticAt.symm` for the inverse function on the domain was not located.

annotations.label: Analytic implies smooth

anchor.snippet: Any analytic function is smooth

annotations.note: `AnalyticAt.contDiffAt` and `AnalyticOnNhd.contDiffOn`/`contDiff` give analytic ⇒ `ContDiff` of any order.

annotations.label: Space of analytic functions is Fréchet

anchor.snippet: is a Fréchet space with respect to the uniform convergence on compact sets

annotations.note: Grep finds no Mathlib statement about a Fréchet topology on the space of analytic functions.

annotations.label: Real analytic functions not complete under compact convergence

anchor.snippet: In contrast, the real analytic functions on an open set are not complete

annotations.note: No corresponding incompleteness result is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Identity theorem

anchor.snippet: This is known as the identity theorem

annotations.note: Stated in `Analytic.IsolatedZeros`/`Uniqueness` as `eqOn_of_preconnected_of_frequently_eq` and `eqOn_of_preconnected_of_eventuallyEq`.

annotations.label: All derivatives zero implies constant

anchor.snippet: if all the derivatives of an analytic function at a point are zero, the function is constant

annotations.note: Follows from the identity theorem in Mathlib but no direct `derivAll = 0 ⇒ const` lemma was located by grep.

annotations.label: Analytic on neighborhood implies smooth

anchor.snippet: any function (real or complex) is infinitely differentiable on a neighborhood where it is equal to a convergent power series

annotations.note: Mathlib's `AnalyticOnNhd.contDiffOn` gives infinite differentiability on the neighborhood of analyticity.

annotations.label: Holomorphic equivalent to complex analytic

anchor.snippet: Any complex-differentiable function

annotations.note: `DifferentiableOn.analyticOnNhd` and `analyticAt_iff_eventually_differentiableAt` establish the equivalence on open sets in `ℂ`.

annotations.label: Liouville's theorem

anchor.snippet: any bounded complex analytic function defined on the whole complex plane is constant

annotations.note: `liouville_theorem_aux` and downstream lemmas in `Analysis/Complex/Liouville.lean` give Liouville's theorem for bounded entire functions.

annotations.label: Complex power series converges on whole open ball

anchor.snippet: if a complex analytic function is defined in an open ball around a point

annotations.note: `HasFPowerSeriesOnBall f p x r` with `r ≤ p.radius` formalizes convergence on the open ball of radius `r`.

annotations.label: Real analytic extends to complex analytic locally

anchor.snippet: Any real analytic function on some open set on the real line can be extended to a complex analytic function

annotations.note: No general construction of a complex-analytic extension of a real-analytic function was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Taylor series converges in neighborhood

anchor.section: Taylor series and radius of convergence
anchor.snippet: If a function is analytic at

annotations.note: `HasFPowerSeriesAt` with `HasFPowerSeriesOnBall.radius_pos` provides convergence on a positive-radius neighborhood.

annotations.label: Radius of convergence

anchor.section: Taylor series and radius of convergence
anchor.snippet: there is a number

annotations.note: `FormalMultilinearSeries.radius` defines the radius of convergence as the supremum of bounded `‖pₙ‖ rⁿ`.

annotations.label: Radius of convergence of a holomorphic function

anchor.section: Taylor series and radius of convergence
anchor.snippet: In complex analysis, the radius of convergence of a holomorphic function at a point is the radius of the largest open disc

annotations.note: Mathlib's radius of convergence applies to a chosen power series; no separate `radiusOfHolomorphy` for the holomorphic function itself was found.

annotations.label: Entire functions have infinite radius of convergence

anchor.section: Taylor series and radius of convergence
anchor.snippet: The series for

annotations.note: `expSeries_radius_eq_top` shows the exp series has radius `∞`; same for `binomialSeries_radius_eq_top_of_nat`.

annotations.label: Complex singularities determine radius on real line

anchor.section: Taylor series and radius of convergence
anchor.snippet: Complex singularities can determine the radius of convergence even for functions that are smooth on the real line

annotations.note: This explanatory remark has no specific Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Binomial series convergence on closed disc without extension

anchor.section: Taylor series and radius of convergence
anchor.snippet: A power series may converge at every point of the boundary of its disc of convergence and still fail to extend holomorphically

annotations.note: `binomialSeries_radius_eq_one` is in Mathlib but boundary-convergence/non-extension claims were not located.

annotations.label: Analytic continuation

anchor.snippet: This process is called analytic continuation

annotations.note: Mathlib has examples of analytic continuation (e.g. `riemannZeta`) but no abstract definition of the continuation process.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of analytic continuation

anchor.snippet: Analytic continuation is unique when it exists on a connected domain

annotations.note: Uniqueness follows from `AnalyticOnNhd.eqOn_of_preconnected_of_eventuallyEq` (the identity theorem) on connected sets.

annotations.label: Logarithm continuation around the origin

anchor.snippet: the natural logarithm is locally analytic on

annotations.note: `Complex.log` is analytic on the slit plane via `analyticAt_clog`, but the multi-valued continuation around 0 isn't formalized.

annotations.label: Algebraic function continuation

anchor.snippet: Algebraic functions give another source of multi-valued analytic continuation

annotations.note: Multi-valued continuation of algebraic functions is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Riemann zeta continuation

anchor.snippet: the Riemann zeta function is initially defined by the Dirichlet series

annotations.note: `differentiableOn_riemannZeta` and its `analyticOnNhd` corollary establish the analytic continuation of `ζ` to `ℂ ∖ {1}`.

annotations.label: Zero sets in several complex variables are not discrete

anchor.snippet: Zero sets of complex analytic functions in more than one variable are never discrete

annotations.note: No statement about non-discreteness of zero sets in several complex variables was located.

annotations.label: Domains of holomorphy in several variables

anchor.snippet: Domains of holomorphy for single-valued functions consist of arbitrary (connected) open sets

annotations.note: Domains of holomorphy and related several-complex-variables theory are not formalized.

annotations.label: Analytic function over complete valued field

anchor.section: Analytic functions over other valued fields
anchor.snippet: is a complete valued field, a function on a neighborhood of a point

annotations.note: `AnalyticAt 𝕜 f x` is defined for `𝕜` a general `NontriviallyNormedField`, covering any complete valued field.

annotations.label: p-adic power series convergence criterion

anchor.section: Analytic functions over other valued fields
anchor.snippet: For example, a power series over

annotations.note: No specific p-adic power series convergence criterion was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convergence on closed disc over non-Archimedean field

anchor.section: Analytic functions over other valued fields
anchor.snippet: More generally, on the closed disc

annotations.note: Non-Archimedean-specific convergence statements on closed discs are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tate algebra

anchor.section: Analytic functions over other valued fields
anchor.snippet: Over a non-Archimedean field the ring of analytic functions on a closed disc is thus related to the Tate algebra

annotations.note: Tate algebras are not defined in Mathlib (grep returned no matches).
